steps.title: Identify the scale factor for each dimension
steps.content: Each dimension increases by 20%, so the new dimension is 120% of the original. This corresponds to a scale factor of $k = 1.2 = \frac{6}{5}$.

steps.title: Apply the area scaling rule to find the new area
steps.content:
When all linear dimensions scale by factor $k$, area scales by factor $k^2$.

The area scaling factor is: $k^2 = (1.2)^2 = \frac{36}{25}$

The new area is:
$$360 \times \frac{36}{25} = \frac{360 \times 36}{25} = \frac{12960}{25} = \frac{2592}{5} \text{ square inches}$$
